data.content:
Both custom targets in `IncrementalBroken.csproj` break incremental builds:

### 1. `GenerateTimestamp` (line 6–11)
- **No `Inputs`/`Outputs`** → MSBuild runs it every build.
- Uses `$([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())` — a **volatile value** that changes every invocation, so the written file always has new content.
- This modifies `build-timestamp.txt` every time, which can cascade into downstream rebuilds.

### 2. `EmbedGitHash` (line 13–23)
- **No `Inputs`/`Outputs`** → always re-runs.
- `WriteLinesToFile … Overwrite="true"` rewrites `git-hash.g.cs` every build, **updating its timestamp even when the git hash hasn't changed**. This forces `CoreCompile` to re-execute because it sees a modified input file.
- `<Compile Include=…>` is added dynamically inside execution, which bypasses evaluation-time up-to-date checks.

### Recommended Fixes

**GenerateTimestamp** — If the timestamp isn't consumed by compilation, gate it with `Inputs`/`Outputs` or remove it. A file whose content changes every build is fundamentally anti-incremental.

**EmbedGitHash** — Add `Inputs`/`Outputs` and use `WriteLinesToFile` with `WriteOnlyWhenDifferent="true"` (or use a two-step write-to-temp + compare-and-copy) so the file's timestamp only changes when the hash actually changes:

```xml
<Target Name="EmbedGitHash"
        BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"
        Inputs="$(MSBuildProjectFullPath)"
        Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs">
  <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
    <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
  </Exec>
  <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
                    Lines="..."
                    Overwrite="true"
                    WriteOnlyWhenDifferent="true" />
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Target>
```

This ensures `CoreCompile` only re-runs when the git hash actually changes.
data.reasoningText:
Let me analyze the two custom targets for incremental build issues.

1. **GenerateTimestamp** target (line 6-11):
   - No `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes → always runs
   - Uses `$([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())` — a volatile property that changes every build
   - Writes a new file every build, which modifies the timestamp file and causes downstream targets to re-run
   - Even if Inputs/Outputs were added, the content changes every time

2. **EmbedGitHash** target (line 13-23):
   - No `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes → always runs
   - Executes `git rev-parse HEAD` on every build and writes the hash file with `Overwrite="true"`, so even unchanged hashes get rewritten and trigger CoreCompile to re-run due to the modified timestamp
   - The dynamic `<Compile Include>` addition compounds the problem

Both targets bypass incremental build caching entirely — one through constantly changing content, the other through unconditional file rewrites that update modification times regardless of actual changes.
